godot_contract_refs.section: SourceEvidence identity, claim-level MechanismSpec traceability, source-audit join, and runtime exclusion
P2 source evidence and source audit disposition join Todo

known_ambiguities: The tracked SourceEvidence authoring set remains empty; this work item's clean Section and test references prove implementation provenance only and are not silently promoted into SourceEvidence records.

Each future external SourceEvidence record must name one exact sealed audit ID and repeat its repository, category, relative path, symbol, file SHA-256, and baseline commit or tree; the compiler never expands all entries that happen to share a file.

The 6,559-entry source audit remains an ignored reproducible P0 artifact. A nonempty SourceEvidence catalog requires its exact sealed bytes locally, while the empty public clone validates the tracked seal without requiring or creating generated data.

The audit's GENERATED_SCOPE_CLASSIFICATION review status is not human evidence review. Authoring independently requires explicit confidence and review status, and only HIGH plus VERIFIED can produce current evidence.

Dirty, missing, stale, deferred, merged, text-only, presentation-only, rejected, and non-applicable audit dispositions remain deterministic blocker codes. Todo 10 owns the later hard release failure for stale or uncovered evidence.

MechanismSpec evidence IDs and behavior claims close bidirectionally. Claims resolve only behavioral JSON Pointer targets and use branch zero for mechanism-wide claims or an existing declared branch ID.

The compiled join contains only IDs, canonical hashes, currentness, counts, and blocker codes. Paths, symbols, summaries, source payloads, timestamps, GUIDs, runtime objects, and absolute paths are excluded.

The join is a read-only non-runtime overlay and does not change the existing spec manifest, runtime catalog, stable-ID domains, maturity promotion, fixture execution, coverage observation, or P0 audit disposition manifest.
Project-requirement-only mechanisms with no evidence IDs are valid and vacuously current for this join; their project requirement and release eligibility remain owned by later gates.
SourceEvidence history is append-only by numeric ID, semantic edits increment evidence_version exactly once, deletion requires a tombstone, and tombstones cannot change or revive.
